== Table Description ==
A classification hierarchy for biological assays, organizing them by therapeutic area, functional classification levels (L1-L3), class type, and data source.

== Column Information ==
[Identifier] assay_class_id: Unique numeric identifiers for assay classes, such as "546", "289", "318". Contains 584 unique identifiers (e.g., "546", "289", "318", "97", "164") with an average length of 2.8 characters.
[TherapeuticArea] l1: Therapeutic area categorization, including NERVOUS SYSTEM, CARDIOVASCULAR SYSTEM, ALIMENTARY TRACT AND METABOLISM. Covers 12 therapeutic areas, including NERVOUS SYSTEM, CARDIOVASCULAR SYSTEM, ALIMENTARY TRACT AND METABOLISM, SYSTEMIC HORMONAL PREPARATIONS, EXCL. SEX HORMONES AND INSULINS, ANTINEOPLASTIC AND IMMUNOMODULATING AGENTS
[FunctionalClassification] l2: Functional classification at level 2, including Tests for Anxiolytic Activity, Anti-Depressant Activity, Cardiovascular Analysis. Functional classifications across 93 categories, including Tests for Anxiolytic Activity, Anti-Depressant Activity, Learning and Memory, Cardiovascular Analysis, Neuroleptic Activity
[FunctionalClassification] l3: Detailed functional classification at level 3, including Computer-Assisted Measurement of Food Consumption in Rats Anorectic Activity, Alloxan Induced Diabetes, Resting Metabolic Rate Anti-Obesity. Functional classifications across 584 categories, including Computer-Assisted Measurement of Food Consumption in Rats Anorectic Activity, Food Consumption in Rats Anorectic Activity, General Anti-Obesity activity, Resting Metabolic Rate Anti-Obesity, Alloxan Induced Diabetes
[FunctionalClassification] class_type: Class type indicating assay validation context, exclusively In vivo efficacy. Functional classifications across 1 categories, including In vivo efficacy
[SourceMetadata] source: Data source identifier, such as Vogel_2008, Hock_2016, phenotype, Vogel_2013_SafetyPharmacology. Contains 4 source identifiers or names, including Vogel_2008, Hock_2016, phenotype, Vogel_2013_SafetyPharmacology

== Table Description ==
Maps target components to their identifiers, biological metadata, and homologue status, likely used for linking biological entities across datasets.

== Column Information ==
[Identifier] tid: Unique identifier for each target component, e.g., 105075, 106082, 10454. Contains 7,839 unique identifiers (e.g., "105075", "106082", "10454", "11123", "102849") with an average length of 5.7 characters.
[BiologicalEntityMetadata] component_id: Biological entity identifier with common values such as 79, 26, 153, representing target components in biological contexts. Biological metadata including 8,070 unique entries, with common values such as 79, 26, 153, 359, 43 and an average length of 3.8 characters.
[Identifier] targcomp_id: Unique identifier for target-component relationships, e.g., 18846, 1961, 3819. Contains 10,000 unique identifiers (e.g., "18846", "1961", "3819", "7321", "18441") with an average length of 4.1 characters.
[BiologicalEntityMetadata] homologue: Homologue status coded as 0, 1, or 2, indicating relationship type to a reference entity. Biological metadata including 3 unique entries, with common values such as 0, 2, 1 and an average length of 1.0 characters.

== Table Description ==
Links mechanism identifiers to external references such as publications, databases, or URLs, enabling traceability of mechanistic data sources.

== Column Information ==
[Identifier] mecref_id: Unique identifier for each mechanism reference entry, e.g., "6890", "8263", "9469". Contains 10,000 unique identifiers (e.g., "6890", "8263", "9469", "11796", "4858") with an average length of 4.2 characters.
[Identifier] mec_id: Identifier for the associated mechanism, e.g., "7408", "6436", "2043". Contains 5,199 unique identifiers (e.g., "7408", "6436", "2043", "483", "4827") with an average length of 3.8 characters.
[ExternalReference] ref_type: Type of external reference source, e.g., "Wikipedia", "PubMed", "Other". Contains 18 external references (e.g., "Wikipedia", "PubMed", "PubMed", "Other", "PubMed") with an average length of 6.0 characters.
[ExternalReference] ref_id: Identifier or DOI of the external reference, e.g., "8799190", "28955978", "https://www.takeda.com/investor-information/results/pdf/2009/qr2009_full_d10_en.pdf". Contains 6,596 external references (e.g., "8799190", "28955978", "17575237", "https://www.takeda.com/investor-information/results/pdf/2009/qr2009_full_d10_en.pdf", "18413790") with an average length of 19.4 characters.
[ExternalReference] ref_url: Full URL linking to the external resource, e.g., "http://europepmc.org/abstract/MED/15886245", "http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Doxazosin", "http://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/lookup.cfm?setid=f1933332-1ae0-4dc1-be4c-694849e6c9f9#nlm34090-...". Contains 6,484 external references (e.g., "http://europepmc.org/abstract/MED/15886245", "http://europepmc.org/abstract/MED/21425998", "http://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/lookup.cfm?setid=f1933332-1ae0-4dc1-be4c-694849e6c9f9#nlm34090-...", "http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Doxazosin", "http://www.futuremedicine.com/doi/abs/10.2217/fon.12.58?url_ver=Z39.88-2003&rfr_id=ori:rid:crossref....") with an average length of 52.5 characters.

== Table Description ==
Lookup table mapping confidence scores to descriptive interpretations and target mapping categories for biological data annotation.

== Column Information ==
[Metadata] confidence_score: Confidence score levels: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 — indicating qualitative certainty tiers for target assignment. Contains 10 metadata entries, including terms like 0, 1, 2, 3, 4. Average length is 1.0 characters.
[Descriptive] description: Descriptive interpretations of target assignment: 'Target assigned is subcellular fraction', 'Target assigned is non-molecular', 'Multiple homologous protein targets may be assigned'. Contains 10 unique descriptions with an average length of 43.4 characters; examples include "Target assigned is subcellular fraction", "Target assigned is non-molecular", "Multiple homologous protein targets may be assigned", "Homologous single protein target assigned", "Direct protein complex subunits assigned".
[BiologicalEntityMetadata] target_mapping: Biological target mapping categories: 'Unassigned', 'Non-molecular', 'Subcellular fraction', 'Molecular (non-protein)', 'Multiple homologous proteins'. Biological metadata including 10 unique entries, with common values such as Unassigned, Non-molecular, Subcellular fraction, Molecular (non-protein), Multiple homologous proteins and an average length of 17.7 characters.

== Table Description ==
A comprehensive dataset linking drugs, substrates, metabolites, and metabolic pathways with enzymatic and taxonomic context, detailing metabolic conversions and biological commentary across human and rat models.

== Column Information ==
[Identifier] met_id: Unique metabolic compound identifiers, such as 2765, 2629, and 2494. Contains 2,147 unique identifiers (e.g., "2765", "2629", "2494", "1009", "2265") with an average length of 3.7 characters.
[Identifier] drug_record_id: Unique drug record identifiers, such as 2472308, 2582882, and 2472636. Contains 273 unique identifiers (e.g., "2472308", "2582882", "2472636", "2582770", "2469405") with an average length of 7.0 characters.
[Identifier] substrate_record_id: Unique substrate record identifiers, such as 2471973, 3460116, and 2472418. Contains 809 unique identifiers (e.g., "2471973", "3460116", "2472418", "2471026", "2470584") with an average length of 7.0 characters.
[Identifier] metabolite_record_id: Unique metabolite record identifiers, such as 2472214, 2469304, and 2582881. Contains 1,599 unique identifiers (e.g., "2472214", "2469304", "2472549", "2582881", "2472499") with an average length of 7.0 characters.
[Identifier] pathway_id: Pathway identifier, consistently valued as 1 across most entries. Contains 5 unique identifiers (e.g., "1", "1", "1", "1", "1") with an average length of 1.0 characters.
[Descriptive] pathway_key: Descriptive pathway labels, such as fig 1, Fig 6A, and fig 8_E_rat. Contains 67 unique descriptions with an average length of 6.7 characters; examples include "fig 1", "Fig 6A", "fig 8_E_rat", "fig 5", "fig 7".
[BiologicalEntityMetadata] enzyme_name: Enzyme names involved in metabolism, such as CYP3A4, CYP2C9, and CYP2D6. Biological metadata including 133 unique entries, with common values such as CYP3A4, CYP2C9, CYP3A, CYP2D6, CYP2C19 and an average length of 8.5 characters.
[Identifier] enzyme_tid: Enzyme target identifiers (TIDs), such as 11365.0, 12866.0, and 17045.0. Contains 89 unique identifiers (e.g., "11365.0", "12866.0", "17045.0", "100637.0", "17045.0") with an average length of 7.2 characters.
[Descriptive] met_conversion: Type of metabolic transformation, such as Reduction of a ketone to a secondary alcohol, Dealkylation, and Glucuronide conjugation at the 8 hydroxyl forming an ether. Contains 433 unique descriptions with an average length of 38.1 characters; examples include "Reduction of a ketone to a secondary alcohol", "Dealkylation", "Glutathione conjugation", "Glucuronide conjugation at the 8 hydroxyl forming an ether.", "Saturation of pyrrole ring".
[Taxonomic] organism: Organism species, such as Rattus norvegicus and Homo sapiens. Taxonomic classifications including 8 unique entries such as "Rattus norvegicus", "Homo sapiens", "Homo sapiens", "Rattus norvegicus", "Homo sapiens", with an average length of 13.1 characters.
[Taxonomic] tax_id: NCBI taxonomic IDs, such as 9606.0 (human) and 10116.0 (rat). Taxonomic classifications including 8 unique entries such as "9606.0", "9606.0", "10116.0", "9606.0", "9606.0", with an average length of 4.2 characters.
[Descriptive] met_comment: Detailed commentary on metabolic conditions or context, such as Organ: Liver|Comment: Shares a common intermediate with M1 in its metabolic pathway., Conditions: in vivo, and Conditions: in-vitro in-vivo conventional rattus norvegicus. Contains 482 unique descriptions with an average length of 93.3 characters; examples include "Organ: Liver|Comment: Shares a common intermediate with M1 in its metabolic pathway. Its formation i...", "Conditions:in vivo", "Conditions: in-vitro in-vivo conventional rattus norvegicus", "Conditions: in vivo", "Conditions:in vivo".

== Table Description ==
Tracks ChEMBL database releases with unique identifiers and creation dates, linking numeric release IDs to their full version strings and timestamps.

[Identifier] chembl_release_id: Numeric identifier for ChEMBL releases, such as 28, 23, and 13. Contains 34 unique identifiers (e.g., "28", "23", "13", "26", "6") with an average length of 1.7 characters.
[Identifier] chembl_release: Full ChEMBL release version string, such as CHEMBL_13, CHEMBL_25, and CHEMBL_32. Contains 34 unique identifiers (e.g., "CHEMBL_13", "CHEMBL_15", "CHEMBL_25", "CHEMBL_6", "CHEMBL_32") with an average length of 8.7 characters.
[Temporal] creation_date: Date when the release was created, ranging from 2009-09-03T00:00 to 2024-03-28T00:00. Temporal data spanning from 2009-09-03T00:00 to 2024-03-28T00:00 across 34 records

== Table Description ==
A dataset of 10,000 predicted protein binding domains with unique identifiers, associated activity and site IDs, prediction method classification, and confidence assessments.

== Column Information ==
[Identifier] predbind_id: Unique numeric identifiers such as 7708069, 7703828, and 7703859, each 7 characters long. Contains 10,000 unique identifiers (e.g., "7708069", "7703828", "7703859", "7707241", "7702559") with an average length of 7.0 characters.

[FunctionalClassification] prediction_method: Prediction method classification into Single domain or Multi domain. Functional classifications across 2 categories, including Single domain, Multi domain
[DataReliability] confidence: Confidence level assessed qualitatively as high or N/A, indicating reliability of the prediction. Contains 2 reliability indicators, including qualitative assessments such as "high", "high", "high", "high", "high". Common themes include N/A.
